title: Cavalier Tunes - II - Give A Rouse
content:
I.
آ آ آ آ King Charles, and whoï؟½ll do him right now?
آ آ آ آ King Charles, and whoï؟½s ripe for fight now?
آ آ آ آ Give a rouse: hereï؟½s, in hellï؟½s despite now,
آ آ آ آ King Charles!

II.
آ آ آ آ Who gave me the goods that went since?
آ آ آ آ Who raised me the house that sank once?
آ آ آ آ Who helped me to gold I spent since?
آ آ آ آ Who found me in wine you drank once?

آ آ آ آ (Chorus.) King Charles, and whoï؟½ll do him right now?
آ آ آ آ King Charles, and whoï؟½s ripe for fight now?
آ آ آ آ Give a rouse: hereï؟½s, in hellï؟½s despite now,
آ آ آ آ King Charles!

III.
آ آ آ آ To whom used my boy George quaff else,
آ آ آ آ By the old foolï؟½s side that begot him?
آ آ آ آ For whom did he cheer and laugh else,
آ آ آ آ While Nollï؟½s damned troopers shot him?

آ آ آ آ (Chorus.) King Charles, and whoï؟½ll do him right now?
آ آ آ آ King Charles, and whoï؟½s ripe for fight now?
آ آ آ آ Give a rouse: hereï؟½s, in hellï؟½s despite now,
آ آ آ آ King Charles!
meaning: About: Robert Browning was a British poet and playwright whose mastery of dramatic verse, especially dramatic monologues, made him one of the foremost Victorian poets.
